explanation: This command uses the 8.3 short name format (e.g., 'Progra~1') to reference a file path, which would trigger the Sigma rule. It simulates an attempt to evade detection by using short names instead of long paths.

This command uses a short name format ('DOCUME~1') in a PowerShell command line, which would match the Sigma rule's detection logic. It demonstrates how short names can be used in different command-line interfaces.

This command uses the 8.3 short name format to access a file path, which would be detected by the Sigma rule. It shows how even standard applications can be used with short names to potentially evade detection.
